ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather record */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time of the weather observation */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station that recorded the data */,
  "temp_c" REAL /* Temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "dew_point_temp_c" REAL /* Dew point temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "rel_humidity" REAL /* Relative humidity in percentage */,
  "wind_dir" INTEGER /* Wind direction in tens of degrees */,
  "wind_spd_kmh" REAL /* Wind speed in kilometers per hour */,
  "visibility_km" REAL /* Visibility in kilometers */,
  "stn_press_kpa" REAL /* Station pressure in kilopascals */,
  "wind_chill" REAL /* Wind chill factor */,
  "weather_condition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather condition */,
  "recorded_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who recorded the data */,
  "condition_severity" INTEGER /* Severity of the weather condition (1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_recorded_by FOREIGN KEY ("recorded_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_weather_condition_id FOREIGN KEY ("weather_condition_id") REFERENCES weather_conditions ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the station in meters */,
  "station_type" TEXT /* Type of the weather station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the station was installed */,
  "maintenance_history" TEXT /* Maintenance history of the station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather condition */,
  "condition_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather condition */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather condition */,
  "severity" INTEGER /* Severity level of the weather condition (1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data contained in the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "alert_name" TEXT /* Name of the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date the alert was generated */,
  "alert_data" TEXT /* Data contained in the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id")
)
